questions.question: Which row describes the arrangement and motion of particles in a solid? arrangement                  motion A random move in all directions, B random stay in one place, C regular                                move                                freely, D regular vibrate about a fixed point
questions.text: Which row describes the arrangement and motion of particles in a solid? arrangement                  motion A random move in all directions, B random stay in one place, C regular                                move                                freely, D regular vibrate about a fixed point
options: A random move in all directions
B random stay in one place
C regular                                move                                freely
D regular vibrate about a fixed point

questions.question: A student measures 25.00 cm 3 of dilute hydrochloric acid accurately. Which apparatus is most suitable? A beaker, B measuring cylinder, C burette, D dropping pipette
questions.text: A student measures 25.00 cm 3 of dilute hydrochloric acid accurately. Which apparatus is most suitable? A beaker, B measuring cylinder, C burette, D dropping pipette
options: A beaker
B measuring cylinder
C burette
D dropping pipette

questions.question: Which sequence is used to separate a soluble salt from a mixture of a soluble and an insoluble salt? A add solvent, heat the mixture, crystallise the mixture, B add solvent, heat the mixture, filter, crystallise the filtrate, C heat the mixture, filter, crystallise the filtrate, D heat the mixture, filter, add solvent, crystallise the filtrate 3
questions.text: Which sequence is used to separate a soluble salt from a mixture of a soluble and an insoluble salt? A add solvent, heat the mixture, crystallise the mixture, B add solvent, heat the mixture, filter, crystallise the filtrate, C heat the mixture, filter, crystallise the filtrate, D heat the mixture, filter, add solvent, crystallise the filtrate 3
options: A add solvent, heat the mixture, crystallise the mixture
B add solvent, heat the mixture, filter, crystallise the filtrate
C heat the mixture, filter, crystallise the filtrate
D heat the mixture, filter, add solvent, crystallise the filtrate 3

questions.question: Which  diagram  shows  the  correct  level  of  the  solvent  at  the  start  of  a  chromatography experiment? B spot of sample level of solvent A spot of sample level of solvent D spot of sample level of solvent C spot of sample level of solvent

questions.question: Which statement about the formation of ions in chemical reactions is correct? A A bromine atom loses an electron and forms a –1 ion., B A chlorine atom gains an electron and forms a –1 ion., C A potassium atom gains an electron and forms a +1 ion., D A sodium atom loses an electron and forms a –1 ion. 4
questions.text: Which statement about the formation of ions in chemical reactions is correct? A A bromine atom loses an electron and forms a –1 ion., B A chlorine atom gains an electron and forms a –1 ion., C A potassium atom gains an electron and forms a +1 ion., D A sodium atom loses an electron and forms a –1 ion. 4
options: A A bromine atom loses an electron and forms a –1 ion.
B A chlorine atom gains an electron and forms a –1 ion.
C A potassium atom gains an electron and forms a +1 ion.
D A sodium atom loses an electron and forms a –1 ion. 4

questions.question: Which row describes the formation of single covalent bonds in methane? A atoms share a pair of electrons both atoms gain a noble gas electronic structure B atoms share a pair of electrons both atoms have the same number of electrons in their outer shell C electrons are transferred from one atom to another both atoms gain a noble gas electronic structure D electrons are transferred from one atom to another both atoms have the same number of electrons in their outer shell
questions.text: Which row describes the formation of single covalent bonds in methane? A atoms share a pair of electrons both atoms gain a noble gas electronic structure B atoms share a pair of electrons both atoms have the same number of electrons in their outer shell C electrons are transferred from one atom to another both atoms gain a noble gas electronic structure D electrons are transferred from one atom to another both atoms have the same number of electrons in their outer shell

questions.question: Which statement explains why graphite is used as a lubricant? A Each carbon atom in graphite forms three bonds., B The bonding in graphite is covalent., C The carbon atoms are arranged in hexagons., D There are weak forces between the layers of carbon atoms.
questions.text: Which statement explains why graphite is used as a lubricant? A Each carbon atom in graphite forms three bonds., B The bonding in graphite is covalent., C The carbon atoms are arranged in hexagons., D There are weak forces between the layers of carbon atoms.
options: A Each carbon atom in graphite forms three bonds.
B The bonding in graphite is covalent.
C The carbon atoms are arranged in hexagons.
D There are weak forces between the layers of carbon atoms.

questions.question: The compound magnesium nitrate has the formula Mg(NO 3 ) 2 . What is the relative formula mass of magnesium nitrate? A 86 B 134 C 148 D 172
questions.text: The compound magnesium nitrate has the formula Mg(NO 3 ) 2 . What is the relative formula mass of magnesium nitrate? A 86 B 134 C 148 D 172

questions.question: Samples of dilute sulfuric acid and concentrated hydrochloric acid are separately electrolysed. Which row describes the product at each electrode during the electrolysis of both substances? product at each anode product at each cathode A different                   different, B different                     same, C same                     different, D same                       same 5
questions.text: Samples of dilute sulfuric acid and concentrated hydrochloric acid are separately electrolysed. Which row describes the product at each electrode during the electrolysis of both substances? product at each anode product at each cathode A different                   different, B different                     same, C same                     different, D same                       same 5
options: A different                   different
B different                     same
C same                     different
D same                       same 5

questions.question: Which row describes the energy level diagram and energy change in an exothermic reaction? energy level diagram energy is A reactants higher than products absorbed, B reactants higher than products released, C reactants lower than products absorbed, D reactants lower than products released
questions.text: Which row describes the energy level diagram and energy change in an exothermic reaction? energy level diagram energy is A reactants higher than products absorbed, B reactants higher than products released, C reactants lower than products absorbed, D reactants lower than products released
options: A reactants higher than products absorbed
B reactants higher than products released
C reactants lower than products absorbed
D reactants lower than products released

questions.question: Which process is a physical change? A a firework exploding, B burning    wood, C chocolate    melting, D iron    rusting
questions.text: Which process is a physical change? A a firework exploding, B burning    wood, C chocolate    melting, D iron    rusting
options: A a firework exploding
B burning    wood
C chocolate    melting
D iron    rusting

questions.question: cm 3 dilute hydrochloric acid balance 1 g calcium carbonate Which statement about the reaction and about changes to the reaction conditions is correct? A Adding    10 cm 3 of water to the 50 cm 3 of acid increases the rate of the reaction., B Increasing the size of the pieces of calcium carbonate increases the rate of the reaction., C Increasing the temperature increases the rate of the reaction., D The mass of the conical flask increases as carbon dioxide is formed. 6 14    When  blue-green  crystals  of  nickel(II)  sulfate  are  heated,  water  is  produced  and  a  yellow  solid remains. When water is added to the yellow solid, the blue-green colour returns. HeNeAr Which row describes these gases? reactivity form of the gas electronic structure, A reactive monoatomic incomplete outer shell of electrons, B unreactive diatomic complete outer shell of electrons, C unreactive diatomic incomplete outer shell of electrons, D unreactive monoatomic complete outer shell of electrons 10 24   The diagrams show the structure of two substances used to make electrical conductors. The results are shown. metal reaction with dilute hydrochloric acid reaction with water R                         reacts                            no                         reaction S no reaction no reaction T                         reacts                         reacts What is the order of reactivity of the metals starting with the most reactive?, A R    → S → T, B S    → R → T, C T    → R → S, D T    → S → R 26   Iron is extracted from its ore in a blast furnace. Hematite, coke, limestone and hot air are added to the furnace. Which explanation is not correct?, A Coke burns and produces a high temperature., B Hematite is the ore containing the iron as iron( III) oxide., C Hot air provides the oxygen for the burning., D Limestone reduces the iron( III) oxide to iron. 11 27   Which property of aluminium makes it useful in the manufacture of aircraft?, A conducts    electricity, B high    boiling    point, C low    density, D silver    colour 28   Water can be treated by filtration then chlorination.
